dataset: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb v1.0.0)
case_id: S001R01 (eyes open baseline), S001R04 (motor imagery) - available for playback, but task uses synthetic data

pi_items.source: OpenBCI_GUI v5.2.2 official documentation and source code verify Spectrogram widget exists in Widget Guide at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/

OpenBCI GUI uses 1-based channel numbering in user interface. Task requires Channel 2. Verified via OpenBCI Forum discussions at https://openbci.com/forum/index.php?p=/discussion/3299/channel-order-in-gui-and-brainflow-confused
OpenBCI_GUI v5.2.2 source code (Widget.pde's ChannelSelect class renders the channel toggle button label as '2', not 'Channel 2'). Metadata claims 'Channel 2' but actual UI shows only '2'.

OpenBCI_GUI v5.2.2 ControlPanel.pde dropdown menu shows 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)'. Web search and documentation at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/ consistently refer to this option, never 'Synthetic (PC)'.

OpenBCI Spectrogram widget documentation confirms it displays a 2D time-frequency heatmap with color gradient. Verified in Widget Guide and community blog at https://openbci.com/community/live-spectrograms-in-processing/
install_openbci.sh specifies PRIMARY_URL for v5.2.2 release from GitHub at https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2

pi_items.verified_value: Real EEG data from PhysioNet eegmmidb v1.0.0 (S001R01.edf, S001R04.edf) is available for playback tasks, licensed under Open Data Commons Attribution License v1.0
pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh downloads from PhysioNet at https://physionet.org/files/eegmmidb/1.0.0/. Verified via https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/

privileged_info_summary: The OpenBCI GUI (v5.2.2) data source dropdown shows 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)', not 'Synthetic (PC)' as claimed in metadata. The Spectrogram widget displays a 2D time-frequency heatmap with color gradient visualization. Channel selection button is labeled '2' (not 'Channel 2'). The environment includes real EEG data from PhysioNet eegmmidb dataset for playback tasks, but this specific task uses algorithmic synthetic data. Key contradiction: metadata falsely claims data_source as 'Synthetic (PC)' when actual GUI option is 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)'.
